title: carex muricata var. contigua
intro: Carex muricata var. contigua, also known as rough sedge or rough-leaved sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that is native to the Pacific coast of North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Carex muricata var. contigua can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
